channel.title: Wildlife on Nature Works
channel.description: Recent content in Wildlife on Nature Works

item.title: Resources
item.description:
Wildlife & forest garden references

Name Details Online Download Forest Garden Spreadsheet All the plants in Martin Crawford’s Creating a Forest Garden. http://localhost:1313/resources/

item.title: UK Wildlife Safari
item.description:
How about a safari park dedicated to UK wildlife?

With news of David Attenborough’s TV series Wild Isles about to hit the airwaves, I was reminded of an idea I had a while back, about a UK Wildlife Safari. http://localhost:1313/blog/230309-uk-safari/

item.title: Vision Pt. 1
item.description:
“So, what does this Nature & Climate Emergency garden actually look like?”

I have always thought that forest gardens are the ideal Climate Emergency garden, ‘tough on Climate Change, tough on the causes of Climate Change’ as Tony Blair (remember him? http://localhost:1313/blog/230227-vision/

item.title: My Wildlife Allotment
item.description:
Nadine Mitschunas has created the most amazing wildlife allotments, and writes a blog the Hardy Plant Society

I first saw My Wildlife Allotment on BBC Gardeners World a couple of years ago, and was impressed then with the visual richness of the allotment. http://localhost:1313/blog/230227-wildlife-allotment/

item.title: Biodiverse
item.description:
Just a couple of quick links, UK & global ecology references

For a long time, I’ve been singing the praises of the Database of Insects and their Food Plants. http://localhost:1313/blog/230209-biodiverse/

item.title: Freebies
item.description:
I have a lot of wildlife & forest garden freebies

My free stuff Forest Garden Spreadsheet - all plants in Creating a Forest Garden Garden Wild Spreadsheet - UK native wild flowers Apple varieties spreadsheet - by Chris Day Plan a Backyard Forest mini-course - basic setup of a forest garden plan Forest garden YouTube channel - design tips, walkthroughs, plants, ideas etc Substack newsletter - weekly updates, links, thoughts, ramblings References Plants For A Future - database of useful plants Plant Atlas - botanical native plant reference for Britain & Ireland RHS plant finder - ornamental plant reference RHS plant finder with native filter DBIF - UK Database of Insects & their Food Plants Database of Pollinator Interactions - pollinator interactions UK native flowers NBN Atlas - National Biodiversity Network of life in the UK GBIF - Global Biodiversity Information Facility, life on Earth Wikimedia Commons - open license images Archive. http://localhost:1313/freebies/

item.title: Benjamin Vogt & Fellowship of the Trees videos
item.description: The two videos have been published! Putting here for reference: http://localhost:1313/blog/230126-videos/

item.title: Gabions
item.description: Gabions are robust wire baskets filled with aggregate or timber. They are relatively easy to install and make fantastic wildlife habitat. http://localhost:1313/gabions/

item.title: Wildlife Forest Garden May 2020
item.description: My next free lockdown forest garden livestream is: The Wildlife Forest Garden Wednesday 6th May, 10am BST twitch. http://localhost:1313/blog/wildlife-forest-garden-may-2020/

item.title: Overview of my forest garden design process
item.description:
A bird’s eye view of my approach to designing a forest garden, in the form of a slideshow and screencast

I’ve been asking friends and colleagues for feedback about what would make a good online course for forest gardening. http://localhost:1313/blog/overview-forest-garden-design-process/

item.title: Wildlife: the power of a name
item.description:
Gardening for others is so much more rewarding, particularly when you know their names

Benjamin Vogt’s @BRVogt article Native Plants, Racism, & the Colonizing Nature of Garden Language is thought provoking stuff. http://localhost:1313/blog/wildlife-power-name/

item.title: Biological recording workshop
item.description:
Clare Flynn ran a fantastic wildlife recording workshop at The People’s Orchard in St Dogmaels, here are my notes to my future self

West Wales Biodiversity Information Centre (WWBIC), made up of Pembrokeshire, Ceredigion & Carmarthen. http://localhost:1313/blog/biological-recording-workshop/

item.title: Wild Food Corridors
item.description:
Involving people in the creation of wildlife habitat in public spaces whilst simultaneously providing a harvest is a way to focus minds on our existential environmental threats

We need to protect and restore our wildlife whilst dramatically reducing our Greenhouse Gas emissions, which means sustainable food production on a global scale, something that can only happen with public awareness, understanding and support. http://localhost:1313/blog/wild-food-corridors/

item.title: DBIF
item.description:
An invaluable tool in the wildlife gardeners’ toolbox, The Database of Insects and their Food Plants details scientifically corroborated interactions between insects and plants

The Database of Insects and their Food Plants (DBIF for short) is a fantastic resource for wildlife gardeners. http://localhost:1313/dbif/

item.title: Bird nest box plan
item.description:
There’s still time to make a bird nesting box for your garden. All you need is 4½ feet of 6x1, a saw, half a dozen screws, a bit of old inner tube and a hole saw.

The whole point of a forest garden is that you are working with nature to grow edible crops. http://localhost:1313/blog/bird-nest-box-plan/

item.title: Nest box
item.description:
Make a bird nesting box for your garden. All you need is 4½ feet of 6"x1" timber, a saw, half a dozen screws, a bit of old inner tube and a hole saw.

The whole point of a forest garden is that you are working with nature to grow edible crops. http://localhost:1313/nest-box/
